title: WHAT I WISH I KNEW BEFORE ATTENDING DREXEL UNIVERSITY
body: I graduated from Drexel 23 yrs ago with a BS in Computer Science. They were charging for printing back then too.  University transfer credit issue you mentioned was a common problem back then also.  Drexel has trimesters (10 weeks) because co-op is two semesters.  Co-op salary really depended on what company you went to, for CS majors they always paid.  Cigna in Philly was paying co-ops very well (had several Drexel interns report to me). Adjunct professors 23 yrs ago were common also, sounds like it hasn’t changed.  Welcome to Philly when it comes to customer service.  Agree with school spirit, it wasn’t too big cept around basketball.  I was part of the ultimate frisbee team.  Can’t relate to your comments about Drexel expanding, the Engineering Building was the new building when I was there.  Regarding Greek life…. Kinda has a mixed background at Drexel since one of the frats had a student fall out a window drunk and kill themselves. Since then the city got stricter with the Greek schools and noise ordinances.  Go Dragons!
author: @JohnTube2K

title: WHAT I WISH I KNEW BEFORE ATTENDING DREXEL UNIVERSITY

I’ve been a nurse for almost 10 years now… but I remember the struggle of just being overloaded with exams every week. They probably do this to help get you prepared for your nursing career.
When I was a new nurse, I was exhausted. You need to figure out time management, working your “3” days a week and then coming home exhausted and going back again.
It does get better!

title: Drexel University [4K] Campus Walking Tour (2021)
body:
15 years ago this did not even look like much of a campus. I can see that there has been an effort to add trees, parks, benches, etc. There also now are lots of stores and the rec center is very popular with students. This campus reminds me of ODU in Norfolk. Drexel is also close to the Amtrak station which is good and close to Center City. Also it looks like there are lots of dorms and apartments in the area.
On the negative side it is still very much a concrete campus. Also there are tons of cars and traffic on campus. Tuition, room and board is $88K for 2026-27 year. That's almost what Yale and Princeton charge ...
author: @13bcoffee
